other_observers: Walter Szeliga, Elena Klaver; I am not the original finder. Walter and Elena were observing the bird as I arrived.
specific_location: Easternmost point of Twin Lakes park where ditch passes under Carter Ct. Approx. 350ft NNW of Carter Ct/Idylwild Trail intersection. GPS lat/lon (40.06311232969484,-105.19322007894516)
description: Small bird, estimated 7 in. head to tail. Black head, upper breast, back, wings, and tail. Undertail coverts and belly white, extending upward to a peak along the breast. Dark eye, thin black bill.Behaviors: Sitting on low bridge rail, fence, and post alongside a ditch/canal. Frequent flycatching activity, sometimes dropping out of sight down into the canal.Call: Occasional call. Slightly downslurred tseeep.Plumage: Adult plumage
photographer: Photographer: Michael Blatchley, Michael Blatchley, Michael Blatchley, Michael Blatchley
